programmes.show_description: What do the Goggleboxers think of Race Across the World, The Last of Us, MAFS Aus, WrestleMania, Chess Masters: The Endgame, Love on the Spectrum, and Secrets of the Penguins?
This time the Goggleboxers watch The Apprentice, Britain's Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, and Alexander Armstrong in India.

programmes.show_name: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programmes.show_description: Lauren, who's 30, has endured a rare cancer called myxoid liposarcoma since she was eight. Can a new surgery fix it and finally allow Lauren to get on with her life?
A car fanatic unloads some of his beloved - and very valuable - classic car collection to raise retirement funds. And could a rare animal pelt keep a cash-strapped innkeeper afloat? (Ep3/3)
Comedian Adam Buxton and Joe Lycett peruse Prague's wonders, as they kayak down the River Vltava, sample sausages out of skulls and caviar-laced ice cream, and enjoy a beer spa.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
In Harrogate, Ilkley and northern Leeds, Kirstie and Phil help two young first-time buyers who are hoping to settle down, and a family who've been scarred by a previous purchase.
Keith Brymer Jones and Marj Hogarth continue their quest to turn a 163-year-old chapel in Pwllheli into their dream home. It's time to rip out the chapel's rot and fungus...
In South Wales, the week's final host Sandra goes heavy on cream and mushrooms, even though none of her guests really like them! But is her night the crème de la crème? (Ep5/5)
Cleanliness has been the buzz word this week, with pretty much all of the hosts unhappy about their less-than-sparkling scores. Have some of them been playing dirty?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: As the season winds down for our Brits abroad, will Abi and Darren's wedding in Spain, and Andrew and Richard's wedding event in France, go off without a hitch?
Debra and Gordon search for a holiday home in Torrevieja for £120k. Debra's keen on apartments, but Gordon wants a townhouse. Can Laura Hamilton find a compromise?

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Ben Hillman catches up with John and Daniel who bought a town house in Cádiz. Jean Johansson visits Kate in Paphos to see how she's getting on in her apartment.
West Midlands. Gemma plans to start the week with an evening of glitz and glamour. But how will her guests react when they find out that she's taken a few shortcuts? (Ep1/5)
West Midlands. Mortgage adviser Andrew aims to 'expand' his guests' minds by adding a pop art dimension to his 1950s beatnik-themed dinner party. (Ep2/5)
West Midlands. Vegetarian Jules hopes to impress her guests with a medieval theme, featuring straw on the floor and a duel. But her medieval menu receives mixed reactions. (Ep3/5)
West Midlands. Well-travelled massage therapist Lucy treats her guests to Thai cuisine, a Thai massage and individual presents. (Ep4/5)
West Midlands. Mental health nurse Lloyd plans a chilled-out night, where his elegant food can take centre stage. (Ep5/5)
Uri and his wife Hazel are keen to find a permanent home in south west Cyprus, to escape the Scottish winters. Can Leah Charles-King find the perfect place for their £200k?
In south London, Kirstie and Phil help Jo and Ally hunt for a family home. And Jennifer is looking for the purr-fect period property with outside space for her cats.
Laura and Nick dream of making a permanent move to Estepona on Spain's Costa del Sol with their beloved dog Poppy. Can Jean Johansson find their perfect place for their £160,000 budget?
...The Final: The finalists create mini model theatres and tackle a tricky throwing technique. Whose final curtain call will crown them as champion?
Roofer Simon has fallen two storeys onto concrete, Evelyn's burnt her finger on a cheese stick, and 97-year-old Doris is in resus with a fast heart rate.

programmes.show_name: Super Surgeons: A Chance at Life
programmes.show_description: Lauren, who's 30, has endured a rare cancer called myxoid liposarcoma since she was eight. Can a new surgery fix it and finally allow Lauren to get on with her life?
The Capture, Married at First Sight Australia and Louis Theroux: Inside the Manosphere are some of the shows on this time. And there's also Will and Ralf Should Know Better.
What do the Goggleboxers think of Race Across the World, The Last of Us, MAFS Aus, WrestleMania, Chess Masters: The Endgame, Love on the Spectrum, and Secrets of the Penguins?
...Experiment: Curious couples sex-periment with opening up their relationships. Connor and Kirstie explore having a threesome. Chanice and Richardo face an unexpected issue.
Horse trainer Claire believes in country air and hard work. Her partner Bambi, a city-loving model and content creator, likes the easy life. Can Bambi keep up with Claire's schedule? (Ep3)
Can an eco-warrior and a petrolhead beauty salon owner agree to disagree on the environment? And a country-loving Yorkshireman shocks a London-based youth worker to the core. (Ep4)
...Less: The Sellers family mealtimes are chaos thanks to dad Rudi's long working hours and daily post-commute supermarket trips. Will they ever get dinner on the table before 9pm?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Cambridge area. The first host, kennel and cattery owner Dean Faulkner, is keen to impress with his cockney-themed evening. (Ep1/5)
Kay Greenwood plans a menu of retro classics in Cambridge. But will her blindfolded taste test entertainment prove hard to swallow? (Ep2/5)
In Cambridge, marketing consultant Matt hopes to deliver the whole package, with a home-grown menu and some outdoor fun and games. And things get competitive at the coconut shy. (Ep3/5)
Cambridge area. Hawa Sydique hosts with an adventurous menu, a funky dress code - and a samba band in her front room. (Ep4/5)
On the final night in Cambridge, Scouser David Goodier hopes to show his southern rivals how to throw a winning dinner party, with a 1960s Merseybeat theme. (Ep5/5)
Portsmouth. The first host, solicitor Allison Hampshire, serves up a sophisticated menu and some quirky entertainment for her guests. (Ep1/5)
Portsmouth. Lecturer Will Dixon's soggy salad starter puts off picky guest Rose Proenca. Can some unexpected entertainment turn his night around? (Ep2/5)
Portsmouth. French tutor Kathy aims to impress her guests with her Gallic-inspired menu. But all does not go to plan in the kitchen, as she battles with a mischievous pineapple! (Ep3/5)
Portsmouth. Stage actor Christopher Marlowe steps into the spotlight with an adventurous menu and a one-man play featuring a drunken waiter and a lot of mess... (Ep4/5)
Portsmouth. Brazilian beauty counter manager Rose treats her guests to some Turkish delights. But things get tricky with her excruciating entertainment. (Ep5/5)
Debra and Gordon search for a holiday home in Torrevieja for £120k. Debra's keen on apartments, but Gordon wants a townhouse. Can Laura Hamilton find a compromise?
Keith Brymer Jones and Marj Hogarth continue their quest to turn a 163-year-old chapel in Pwllheli into their dream home. It's time to rip out the chapel's rot and fungus...
Keith and Marj turn the old schoolroom into a snug, complete with a baroque chair and feature wall, before discovering Capel Salem's fiery past at the National Library of Wales.
Taking inspiration from Coventry Cathedral, Keith and Marj create a Victorian-style parlour bathroom, complete with a giant candlestick holder and Marj's dream lampshade.
Kirsty wants a holiday home on the Canary Island of Lanzarote for her budget of £120,000. With her best pal Wendy joining her on the search, can Craig Rowe find Kirsty her perfect place?
Jo and DP look for a holiday home in Nerja on Spain's ever-popular Costa del Sol, to escape to in the winter and rent out in the summer. Can Ben Hillman deliver their dream?

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Alnwick, Kirstie and Phil meet ministers Chris and Caroline, who need a divine intervention. Caroline loves their home and neighbours, but Chris craves more space.
Epic rags-to-riches drama, based on Barbara Taylor Bradford's bestseller, charting Emma Harte's rise from maid to mogul. With Brenda Blethyn, Jessica Reynolds and Emmett J. Scanlan. (Ep1/8)
Epic rags-to-riches drama with Brenda Blethyn, Jessica Reynolds and Emmett J. Scanlan. Emma and Edwin's forbidden love deepens, but can they ever overcome their class differences? (Ep2/8)
Scott's rushed into Queen's Medical Centre by air ambulance after crashing his motorbike at 100mph. And Peter has been crushed by an eight-tonne static caravan.
